items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical vulnerabilities, including CVE-2026-48358 (unauthenticated arbitrary code execution in webhooks) and CVE-2026-48356 (unrestricted file upload). Adobe has released isolated patches, and exploitation in the wild is not yet known.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer allows remote code execution via a crafted CacheWarmer cookie. All versions before 1.11.12 are affected, and CISA has added it to its KEV catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This security update addresses multiple critical vulnerabilities, including stored Cross-site Scripting (XSS) leading to arbitrary code execution (CVE-2026-34686) and a Path Traversal vulnerability allowing arbitrary file system writes (CVE-2026-34653). It affects Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: PolyShell, identified as CVE-2026-34653, is a critical flaw allowing unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to Magento stores via the REST API. Adobe Commerce 2.4.9 contains a fix, and active exploitation has been observed.
